title: Neocouma parviflora
taxonomy: Neocouma parviflora is classified within the plant family Annonaceae. This family comprises over 2,000 species of flowering plants, commonly known as custard apples or soursops. The Annonaceae family is found in tropical regions, including Africa, Asia, and the Americas. Neocouma parviflora is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Cameroon and Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of Neocouma parviflora is further detailed within the genus Neocouma, which is a genus of flowering plants within the Annonaceae family.
uses: Neocouma parviflora has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are utilized to treat various health conditions. Its extracts are also used to manage fever and to treat skin infections. Moreover, Neocouma parviflora is believed to have antiseptic properties, making it effective against bacterial and fungal infections. Further research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses of Neocouma parviflora.
